title: Betula trichogemma
intro: Betula trichogemma, also known as the paper birch, is a deciduous tree species native to the mountainous regions of East Asia. The bark of Betula trichogemma contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenes, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and gastrointestinal issues. The health benefits of Betula trichogemma include its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which can help protect against infections, reduce inflammation, and neutralize free radicals in the body. In traditional medicine, Betula trichogemma is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used in the form of a topical salve or ointment to treat skin conditions and wounds.

preparations: Betula trichogemma, also known as the Korean paper birch, is a species of birch tree native to East Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes, including the treatment of fever and inflammation. Herbal preparations made from the tree's bark and leaves are said to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
